experiment: b=7 corrected-onset witness, tetraquadric 3+1 family (tau(7)=20 != rho(7)=21)
role: decisive distinction between the old cubic-crossing threshold tau(b) and the corrected source-target onset rho(b); see Corollary 5.2 and Section 9 of the paper
method: defect = Theorem 5.1 source-target formula; matrix dimensions confirmed by direct construction (build_sparse). At both depths the defect is now confirmed by a randomized sparse finite-field computation (Wiedemann/Kaltofen-Saunders: exact arithmetic mod p, randomized rank recovery) at two independent primes each; see scripts/wiedemann_rank.py and logs/b7_d{20,21}_sparse_p{100003,100019}.txt. The dense deterministic F_p rank (scripts/run_b7_decisive.py) remains pending on an adequate-memory host and is the archival record this note ultimately targets.

sparse_confirmation.method: randomized sparse finite-field rank (Wiedemann/Kaltofen-Saunders); exact arithmetic mod p, randomized rank recovery

(20,20,20).rank_status: Dense deterministic F_p rank of a 54872 x 55566 matrix (~24 GB as flint.nmod_mat) still PENDING on an adequate-memory host; run scripts/run_b7_decisive.py on a >= ~32 GB host. Confirmed in the interim by the randomized sparse computation above at two primes.

sparse_confirmation.method: randomized sparse finite-field rank (Wiedemann/Kaltofen-Saunders); exact arithmetic mod p, randomized rank recovery

(21,21,21).rank_status: Dense deterministic F_p rank of a 64000 x 63888 matrix (~32 GB as flint.nmod_mat) still PENDING on an adequate-memory host; run scripts/run_b7_decisive.py on a >= ~40 GB host. Confirmed in the interim by the randomized sparse computation above at two primes.

interpretation: At d=20 the old tau(7)=20 would call the defect saturated at the ceiling C_7=2688; the source-target law gives 1994, strictly below the ceiling, so saturation is not reached until d=21=rho(7). The predicted values follow from Theorem 5.1, whose two branches were independently reproduced over F_p at b=4 (480) and b=5 (48,158,352,642,960) in this release, and are now also confirmed at b=7 by the randomized sparse computation at both depths and both primes (1994 at d=20, 2688 at d=21).
reproduce: python3 scripts/run_b7_decisive.py --check                 # dims only; runs anywhere
python3 scripts/run_b7_decisive.py --depths 20 --primes 100003 100019   # full dense rank; needs ~32 GB
python3 scripts/run_b7_decisive.py --depths 21 --primes 100003 100019   # full dense rank; needs ~40 GB
python3 scripts/wiedemann_rank.py --bd 7 20 100003          # randomized sparse rank; runs on commodity hardware
python3 scripts/wiedemann_rank.py --b7                      # all four sparse points, revalidated against archived dense ranks first
python3 scripts/verify_v04.py                              # checks the formula and any present logs
python3 scripts/verify_v04.py --require-b7                 # also fails if b=7 dense logs absent
status: proven by Theorem 5.1; matrix dimensions verified by construction; confirmed at b=7 by a randomized sparse finite-field computation at two primes per depth (exact arithmetic mod p, randomized rank recovery); the dense deterministic rank remains pending on an adequate-memory host as the archival record
